target: Decouple Pinky and Shodh — Hosaka fork plan with upstream contributions

executive_summary.key_insight: Don't fork. Decouple at the DATA layer (SlowStore/SQLite), not the CODE layer (git fork). The novel IP is ~400 lines in 5 files — extract it as a Hosaka extension module that depends on upstream shodh, reads topology via SlowStore concurrent access, and writes back computed insights via HTTP.
the_plan: 1. Contribute standard improvements to shodh upstream, tag v0.8.0
2. Create Hosaka repo that depends on shodh v0.8.0 (git tag, not fork)
3. Novel IP (~400 lines) lives in Hosaka as extension modules
4. Hosaka reads shodh's SlowStore (SQLite WAL) for topology — zero-copy, no HTTP overhead
5. Hosaka writes computed insights back via shodh's HTTP API (lightweight scores/signals)
6. Pinky remains a separate project, already HTTP-decoupled, unchanged
7. TUI stays in shodh repo but adds optional Hosaka panel when Hosaka API is available

lens_a_edgewalk.top_finding: Vendored binary at pinky/ext/shodh-memory is v0.1.91 — 6 major versions behind. Fork approach already failed empirically (severity 8).
key_boundaries: Pinky↔Shodh: HTTP API only, zero compile-time coupling
Implicit JSON contracts (GapAnalysisResult, VoronoiResult) with no shared types
Overlook state stored as OVERLOOK_CONCEPTUAL_SELF: prefix in shodh memories — reverse dependency
EdgeSource::CollectivePrior is a breaking enum variant for persisted data
PiiClassification not backfilled on existing entities
graph_retrieval signature change creates merge conflict surface

lens_b_hde.top_finding: Fork is the wrong metaphor — Hosaka is a consumer of graph topology, not a modifier of storage. SlowStore (SQLite WAL) is the natural decoupling interface.
tangents_followed: Fork vs wrapper vs sidecar — sidecar via SlowStore wins
Hebbian graph entanglement — membrane function in shodh, policy in Hosaka
Speed from data colocation, not code colocation
Git history cannot cleanly separate interleaved IP
TUI is the canary for decoupling quality
lens_b_hde.unifying_insight: The selective membrane principle applies to CODEBASE architecture, not just DATA architecture. SlowStore = membrane. Topology flows out (read-only). Computed insights flow in (HTTP push).

lens_c_clustering.top_finding: The fork boundary is within shodh files at block/line level, not between repos. ~400 lines of novel IP in graph_memory.rs, state.rs, decay.rs, constants.rs, auth.rs.

clusters.S_upstream: Pure substrate (storage, embeddings, vector search, types) — ~450K lines, clean boundary
clusters.H_hosaka: Novel IP (~400 lines mixed into 5 files) — CollectiveStore, PII policy, decay multipliers, edge export
clusters.P_pinky: Cognitive orchestrator — entirely HTTP-decoupled, zero type coupling, clean boundary
clusters.T_interface: TUI, MCP, hooks — separate processes, clean boundary

missing_mediators: trait PiiPolicy — pluggable PII classification policy
trait ExternalScoreProvider — generic external quality signal push
trait WeightPersistence — pluggable weight serialization strategy

ab_products.finding: Vendored fork at ext/shodh-memory (v0.1.91, 6 versions behind) is empirical proof that forking creates maintenance debt. SlowStore interface avoids this.
Overlook state + PinkyScores = two ad-hoc implementations of the same bidirectional protocol. Formalize as ExternalScoreProvider + ContextBlocks.
JSON contract drift (sev 7) only applies to lightweight HTTP write-back path. Heavy reads go through SlowStore (no JSON serialization).

ac_products.finding: C's line-precise mapping of ~400 lines novel IP in 5 files gives exact cherry-pick boundaries for A's upstream PR.
C's 5 missing mediator traits ARE the API contract A found missing. Traits = compile-time contract enforcement.

bc_products.finding: SlowStore = C's cluster boundary between substrate and novel IP = B's data interface. Same boundary seen from three angles.
Orphaned ext/shodh-memory proves shodh-core extraction is premature. Contribute-then-tag-then-depend instead.
Rename PinkyDimensionScores → ExternalDimensionScores as first commit before any other work.

phase_2_resonances.name: Novel IP Is ~400 Lines, Not a Fork
phase_2_resonances.three_body: upstream shodh × Hosaka × Pinky on graph_memory.rs changes

R1_fix.name: SlowStore schema contract with versioning
R1_fix.final_fix: Add schema_version table to SlowStore. Upstream shodh = v1 (base tables). Hosaka extends to v2+ (collective topology, Voronoi centroids, interference events). Hosaka reads via WAL concurrent readers.

R2_fix.name: Hosaka extension module, not fork
R2_fix.final_fix: Create hosaka/src/extensions/ that imports shodh types and adds CollectiveStore, pii_aware_decay, export_collective_edges. Upstream PR includes EdgeSource::CollectivePrior as inert variant + #[serde(other)] for future-proofing.

R3_fix.name: Contribute → tag → depend workflow
R3_fix.final_fix: PR standard changes to shodh main. Tag v0.8.0. Hosaka Cargo.toml: shodh-memory = { git = '...', tag = 'v0.8.0' }. CI builds Hosaka against shodh main nightly.

R4_fix.name: ExternalScoreProvider + ContextBlocks
R4_fix.final_fix: Rename PinkyDimensionScores → ExternalDimensionScores. Rename handlers/pinky.rs → handlers/external_dimensions.rs. Move Overlook state from memory prefix-hack to /api/context/blocks.

ranked_actions.action: Rename PinkyDimensionScores → ExternalDimensionScores and handlers/pinky.rs → handlers/external_dimensions.rs
ranked_actions.rationale: Prerequisite — removes naming coupling before any upstream PR

ranked_actions.action: PR standard changes to shodh-memory main branch, tag v0.8.0
ranked_actions.rationale: 3-lens resonance (R3). Enables Hosaka to depend on versioned shodh instead of forking.
ranked_actions.effort: MEDIUM (clean up diff, squash, test, PR)

ranked_actions.action: Create Hosaka repo with shodh-memory as git tag dependency
ranked_actions.rationale: 3-lens resonance (R2). ~400 lines of novel IP as extension module, NOT a 500K-line fork.
hosaka_modules: extensions/maintenance.rs — weight aggregation, Moka eviction persistence

ranked_actions.action: Formalize SlowStore as the data interface contract
ranked_actions.rationale: 3-lens resonance (R1). SlowStore = the selective membrane between shodh substrate and Hosaka intelligence.
ranked_actions.effort: MEDIUM (schema versioning, collective tables, Hosaka reader)
schema_additions: collective_edges table (from_name, to_name, strength, relation_type, contributing_users)
population_weights table (bm25, vector, graph, contributing_users, updated_at)

ranked_actions.action: Move Overlook state from memory prefix-hack to /api/context/blocks
ranked_actions.rationale: 2-lens resonance (R4). Current OVERLOOK_CONCEPTUAL_SELF: prefix is brittle.
ranked_actions.effort: LOW (Pinky side: change 2 HTTP calls. Shodh side: already has ContextBlockStore)

ranked_actions.rationale: 2-lens resonance. ext/shodh-memory at v0.1.91 is orphaned, 6 versions behind.
ranked_actions.effort: LOW (delete ext/shodh-memory, update shodh_server.rs to find binary via PATH or config)

ranked_actions.action: Add migration to backfill PiiClassification on existing entities
ranked_actions.rationale: Without backfill, is_edge_safe_for_collective returns true for all edges (vacuously — all entities default to Clean)
ranked_actions.effort: LOW (~20 lines in maintenance cycle: iterate entities, call from_labels, persist)

ranked_actions.action: Add /version endpoint to shodh for capability discovery
ranked_actions.rationale: Pinky and Hosaka need to know which shodh version they're talking to

what_does_NOT_get_forked: RocksDB storage, Vamana index, embedders, BM25, compression, MIF
what_lives_in_hosaka: Maintenance weight aggregation + Moka eviction persistence (~30 lines)
Future: Voronoi atlas, interference clustering, contested gaps, behavioral archetypes (~TBD)
what_lives_in_pinky: Overlook engine, Bifocal+, FourEyes, persistent homology, distortions
HTTP calls to shodh API (already clean)
Future: HTTP calls to Hosaka API for novel intelligence signals

orthogonality_score.note: High — Lens A found operational coupling, B found architectural metaphor, C found code-level topology. Minimal overlap.
